overall_listening.Pre-A1: Can recognize everyday, familiar words, provided they are delivered clearly and slowly in a clearly defined, familiar everyday context.
overall_listening.A1: Can follow speech which is very slow and carefully articulated, with long pauses for him/her to assimilate meaning.
overall_listening.A2: Can understand enough to be able to meet needs of a concrete type provided speech is clearly and slowly articulated.
overall_listening.B1: Can understand straightforward factual information about common everyday or job related topics, identifying both general messages and specific details, provided speech is clearly articulated in a generally familiar accent.
overall_listening.B2: Can understand the main ideas of propositionally and linguistically complex speech on both concrete and abstract topics delivered in a standard dialect, including technical discussions in his/her field of specialization.
overall_listening.C1: Can understand enough to follow extended speech on abstract and complex topics beyond his/her own field, though he/she may need to confirm occasional details, especially if the accent is unfamiliar.
overall_listening.C2: Has no difficulty in understanding any kind of spoken language, whether live or broadcast, delivered at fast native speed.

overall_reading.Pre-A1: Can recognize familiar names, words and very basic phrases on simple notices in the most common everyday situations.
overall_reading.A1: Can understand very short, simple texts a single phrase at a time, picking up familiar names, words and basic phrases and rereading as required.
overall_reading.A2: Can understand short, simple texts on familiar matters of a concrete type which consist of high frequency everyday or job-related language.
overall_reading.B1: Can read straightforward factual texts on subjects related to his/her field and interest with a satisfactory level of comprehension.
overall_reading.B2: Can read with a large degree of independence, adapting style and speed of reading to different texts and purposes, and using appropriate reference sources selectively.
overall_reading.C1: Can understand in detail lengthy, complex texts, whether or not they relate to his/her own area of speciality, provided he/she can reread difficult sections.
overall_reading.C2: Can understand and interpret critically virtually all forms of the written language including abstract, structurally complex, or highly colloquial literary and non-literary writings.

online_interaction.Pre-A1: Can post simple online greetings and short messages using basic formulaic expressions.
online_interaction.A1: Can post simple online greetings, short messages, and very short statements about himself/herself.
online_interaction.A2: Can engage in basic social interaction online (e.g. simple greetings, thanks, short comments), writing very short, simple notes and emails.
online_interaction.B1: Can post online accounts of social events, experiences and activities referring to embedded links and media and sharing personal feelings.
online_interaction.B2: Can engage actively in online discussions, stating and responding to opinions on topics of interest at some length, provided contributors avoid unusual or complex language.
online_interaction.C1: Can express him/herself with clarity and precision in real-time online interaction, adjusting language flexibly and effectively for social, academic or professional purposes.
online_interaction.C2: Can participate effectively in live online professional or academic discussion, asking for and giving further clarification of complex, abstract issues as necessary.

mediation_text.A1: Can convey simple, predictable information of immediate interest given in short, simple texts on familiar matters, provided he/she can read the text several times.
mediation_text.A2: Can convey the main point(s) of a short, simple text on everyday subjects of immediate interest provided he/she can read it clearly.
mediation_text.B1: Can convey information given in clear, well-structured informational texts on subjects that are familiar or of personal or current interest.
mediation_text.B2: Can convey the main points made in long texts expressed in clear standard language on topics of personal or professional interest.
mediation_text.C1: Can convey the main points and relevant details of complex texts, whether or not they relate to his/her own professional field.
mediation_text.C2: Can explain the subtle distinctions and implications of complex texts, even those on abstract or unfamiliar topics.

plurilingual_comprehension.A1: Can understand short, simple words and phrases by using the similarities between the target language and other languages he/she knows.
plurilingual_comprehension.A2: Can exploit easily identifiable vocabulary (e.g. internationalisms) to understand the general idea of short, simple texts.
plurilingual_comprehension.B1: Can deduce the meaning of unknown words by using his/her knowledge of the vocabulary and grammar of other languages he/she knows.
plurilingual_comprehension.B2: Can use his/her knowledge of other languages to understand the main points of a text on a familiar topic, even if he/she does not know the target language well.
plurilingual_comprehension.C1: Can effectively use his/her plurilingual repertoire to understand complex texts and interactions, switching languages where appropriate.
plurilingual_comprehension.C2: Can mediate effectively between languages and cultures, exploiting his/her full plurilingual repertoire to facilitate communication in complex situations.

item_writing: Distractors must be indisputably wrong, not just less likely.
Items must be answerable solely from the provided text.
Independence: One item's success must not depend on another.
